entries.first_line_text: | While-loop variant proof-expansion surface | `tests/build/pr1123b_while_variant_patterns_build.safe` | PR11.23b extends the derivable while-loop variant surface with positive string `.length` guards and downward integer countdown guards, while keeping MIR validation and Ada emission in lockstep. The fixture covers plain and bounded string length decrease, `> 0` and `>= 1` guards, strict and inclusive literal countdowns, arbitrary literal lower bounds, strict and inclusive constant two-identifier countdown bounds, and literal-left mirror forms under the blocking emitted-proof lane. Mutable right-bound identifiers are rejected by `tests/negative/neg_while_variant_mutable_bound.safe`, and array/list length drains are rejected by `tests/negative/neg_while_variant_array_length_drain.safe` until runtime contracts expose strict-decrease facts. | yes | yes | yes | yes | yes | none | no |

The emitted invariant uses the maximum static append length across conditional branches (`"yes"` vs. `"no"`) plus bounded-string capacity facts so generated `To_Bounded` preconditions prove at level 2. | yes | yes | yes | yes | yes | none | no |

entries.first_line_text: | Multiple independent accumulator proof-expansion surface | `tests/build/pr1123e_multi_accumulator_build.safe` | PR11.23e emits independent fail-closed loop invariants for multiple integer accumulators in a growable `for of` body. PR11.23f intentionally extends this fixture with the sum/count relational invariant because it contains both `count = count + 1` and `sum = sum + item`. The fixture also covers an intentionally skipped non-additive `max_seen = item` update. | yes | yes | yes | yes | yes | none | no |

entries.first_line_text: | Scoped sum/count relational proof-expansion surface | `tests/build/pr1123f_sum_count_relational_build.safe`, `tests/negative/neg_pr1123f_unguarded_sum_count.safe` | PR11.23f emits fail-closed relational invariants for the guarded average pattern: one exact top-level `count = count + 1` counter, no recursive writes or shadowing, and one distinct bounded nonnegative sum accumulator. The positive fixture proves a bounded `sum / count` conversion under an explicit `count > 0` guard; the negative fixture pins unguarded post-loop `sum / count` as a clean frontend rejection pending #321. | yes | yes | yes | yes | yes | Unguarded post-loop nonzero inference remains tracked as #321. | no |

entries.first_line_text: | Fixed-depth nested iteration proof-expansion surface | `tests/build/pr1123g_nested_iteration_build.safe` | PR11.23g emits fail-closed composed accumulator invariants for the fixed two-level nested `for of` shape where the inner iterable is a literal-backed local growable collection with a known static length and the inner update has a bounded nonnegative product delta. The fixture proves `total = total + x * y` across local literal-backed `xs` and `ys` lists at level 2, and pins that an unrelated inner loop does not suppress an outer accumulator invariant. The supporting progress conjunct is intentionally shared by all growable accumulator invariants, so adjacent proof-expansion fixtures such as `pr213_map_entry_build.safe`, `pr1123e_multi_accumulator_build.safe`, and `pr1123f_sum_count_relational_build.safe` also receive emitted-Ada snapshot churn. Dynamic inner lengths, arbitrary-depth nesting, and type-capacity inference remain out of scope. | yes | yes | yes | yes | yes | none | no |

entries.first_line_text: | Shared call-argument snapshot proof-expansion surface | `tests/build/pr1123h_shared_call_argument_snapshot_build.safe` | PR11.23h generalizes the existing shared-condition snapshot replacement pass into assignment RHS and standalone call-argument contexts. The fixture pins the original compound-condition #287 shape, then proves flat and nested shared getter reads passed through function call arguments and `print` without exposing volatile getter calls to GNATprove's interfering-context checks. The existing one-snapshot-per-shared-root discipline is preserved, so multiple field reads from the same shared object use one whole-root snapshot local per statement. | yes | yes | yes | yes | yes | none | no |

entries.first_line_text: | Clamp-style exported postcondition proof-expansion surface | `tests/build/pr1123i_clamp_provider.safe`, `tests/build/pr1123i_exported_postconditions_build.safe` | PR11.23i emits conservative branch-equality `Post` aspects on generated package specs for pure integer clamp-style functions. The checkpoint imports `pr1123i_clamp_provider.clamp`, assigns its result into a positive constrained subtype, and proves the downstream division after GNATprove uses the imported spec contract. The emitted contract intentionally describes branch behavior rather than claiming an unconditional result range, so calls with `lo > hi` remain sound. General strongest-postcondition inference, heap/shared reads, calls, loops, mutation, and arithmetic beyond unary/binary `+`/`-` stay out of scope. | yes | yes | yes | yes | yes | none | no |
